title: श्री शुक्र सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री शुक्र सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥

हिमकुन्दमृणालाभं दैत्यानां परमं गुरुम्।
सर्वशास्त्रप्रवक्तारं भार्गवं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥

श्वेतवर्णं चतुर्बाहुं श्वेतवस्त्रविभूषितम्।
दण्डं रुद्राक्षं कमण्डलुं जपमालां करे धृतम्।
श्वेताश्वरथमारूढं शुक्रं वन्दे दैत्यगुरुम्॥

॥ अथ शुक्र सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् ॥

शुक्रो भार्गवो काव्यो दैत्यगुरुर्महायशाः।
शनैश्चरमित्रश्चैव शुक्लवासा महाप्रभुः॥ १॥

भृगुपुत्रो भृगुवंश्यो ज्योतिषां पतिरुत्तमः।
संजीवनीविद्यापारो मृतसंजीवनक्षमः॥ २॥

तुलाधीशो वृषभेशः कामदो भोगवर्धनः।
वैभवप्रदमार्गज्ञो सौन्दर्यकारको ग्रहः॥ ३॥

स्त्रीसुखदो दाम्पत्यकारको विवाहदो बली।
कलाप्रियो सौख्यदश्च रतिप्रदो मनोहरः॥ ४॥

वाहनप्रदमार्गज्ञो ऐश्वर्यदो धनप्रदः।
सुगन्धप्रियदेवश्च सुवासितमनोहरः॥ ५॥

हीरकप्रभसम्पन्नो रजतप्रभवो ग्रहः।
पूर्वदिक्पालमित्रश्चैव श्वेतपुष्पप्रियो मुनिः॥ ६॥

दैत्यानां चक्षुर्दातृ देवासुरसमर्चितः।
कबन्धभीषणं हत्वा विष्णुकृपासमाश्रितः॥ ७॥

असुराणां हितार्थी च सदा न्यायपरायणः।
नीतिशास्त्रकरः श्रेष्ठो राजनीतिविशारदः॥ ८॥

कामेश्वरसमो रूपे रम्भासमप्रभो ग्रहः।
मोहनास्त्रविदां नाथो वशीकरणदायकः॥ ९॥

सप्तवर्णधरः श्वेतो धवलो निर्मलप्रभः।
सौभाग्यकारको मान्यो विलासी कान्तिवर्धनः॥ १०॥

गृहसुखकरो दिव्यो भवनदो सम्पदां प्रदः।
यानवाहनदश्चैव राज्यभोगविवर्धनः॥ ११॥

वेदवेदाङ्गतत्त्वज्ञो सर्वशास्त्रविशारदः।
मन्त्रवेत्ता तन्त्रवेत्ता ज्योतिःशास्त्रविचक्षणः॥ १२॥

काव्यादर्शप्रवक्ता च साहित्यरसिको ग्रहः।
सङ्गीतविद्यानिपुणो नृत्यकलासु पण्डितः॥ १३॥

शुक्रवारेश्वरो मान्यो शुक्रमन्त्रजपप्रियः।
श्वेतधातुरत्नदश्च वज्रमणिप्रभाकरः॥ १४॥

दशमस्थानराज्यदो सप्तमे कामसुखदः।
चतुर्थे गृहवाहनदो द्वितीये धनसम्पदम्॥ १५॥

लग्ने कान्तिसुखदश्च पञ्चमे पुत्रसम्पदम्।
त्रिकोणे शुभदश्चैव केन्द्रे सम्पत्प्रदो ग्रहः॥ १६॥

आयुर्वेदविदां श्रेष्ठो रसायनविशारदः।
चिकित्साशास्त्रकुशलो सर्वरोगनिवारकः॥ १७॥

मधुदैत्यगुरुश्चैव प्रह्लादोपासिता ग्रहः।
शिवभक्तो विष्णुभक्तो ब्रह्मविद्याविशारदः॥ १८॥

महापण्डितसम्पन्नो दीर्घकेशो तपस्विनाम्।
योगसिद्धिकरो मान्यो ध्यानसिद्धिप्रदो ग्रहः॥ १९॥

सर्वसम्पत्प्रदो देवो सर्वसौख्यविवर्धनः।
सर्वकामप्रदो मान्यो सर्वमङ्गलकारकः॥ २०॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥

शुक्रसहस्रनाम्नां तु पठनाद्यत्फलं लभेत्।
सौन्दर्यं धनमैश्वर्यं सुखं विवाहमाप्नुयात्॥

शुक्रवारे तु यः पठेन्नियमेन समाहितः।
शुक्रदोषविनिर्मुक्तो भोगैश्वर्यमवाप्नुयात्॥

दाम्पत्यसुखमाप्नोति कलाविद्यासु निपुणः।
षण्मासपठनाद्भक्त्या सर्वसिद्धिमवाप्नुयात्॥

॥ इति श्री स्कन्दपुराणोक्तं श्री शुक्र सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Shukra Sahasranama Stotram ||

|| Dhyanam ||

Hima-kunda-mrinala-abham Daityanam paramam Gurum |
Sarva-shastra-pravaktaram Bhargavam pranamami-aham ||

Shveta-varnam Chatur-bahum Shveta-vastra-vibhushitam |
Dandam Rudraksha Kamandulum Japamalam kare dhritam |
Shveta-ashva-ratham-arudham Shukram vande Daitya-gurum ||

|| Atha Shukra Sahasranama Stotram ||

Shukro Bhargavo Kavyo Daitya-gurur-Maha-yashah |
Shanaishchara-mitrash-chaiva Shukla-vasa Maha-prabhuh || 1 ||

Bhrigu-putro Bhrigu-vamshyo Jyotisham patir-uttamah |
Sanjivanee-vidya-paro Mrita-sanjivana-kshamah || 2 ||

Tula-adhisho Vrishabheshah Kama-do Bhoga-vardhanah |
Vaibhava-prada-marga-jno Saundarya-karako Grahah || 3 ||

Stri-sukha-do Dampatya-karako Vivaha-do Bali |
Kala-priyo Saukhya-dash-cha Rati-prado Manoharah || 4 ||

Vahana-prada-marga-jno Aishvarya-do Dhana-pradah |
Sugandha-priya-devash-cha Suvasita-manoharah || 5 ||

Hiraka-prabha-sampanno Rajata-prabhavo Grahah |
Purva-dik-pala-mitrash-chaiva Shveta-pushpa-priyo Munih || 6 ||

Daityanam chakshur-datri Deva-asura-samarchitah |
Kabandha-bhishanam hatva Vishnu-kripa-samashritah || 7 ||

Asuranam hita-arthi cha sada Nyaya-parayanah |
Niti-shastra-karah shreshtho Rajaniti-visharadah || 8 ||

Kameshwara-samo rupe Rambha-sama-prabho Grahah |
Mohana-astra-vidam natho Vashi-karana-dayakah || 9 ||

Sapta-varna-dharah Shveto Dhavalo Nirmala-prabhah |
Saubhagya-karako Manyo Vilasi Kanti-vardhanah || 10 ||

Griha-sukha-karo Divyo Bhavana-do Sampadam pradah |
Yana-vahana-dash-chaiva Rajya-bhoga-vivardhanah || 11 ||

Veda-vedanga-tattvajno Sarva-shastra-visharadah |
Mantra-vetta Tantra-vetta Jyotih-shastra-vichakshanah || 12 ||

Kavya-adarsha-pravakta cha Sahitya-rasiko Grahah |
Sangita-vidya-nipuno Nritya-kalasu Panditah || 13 ||

Shukravara-ishwaro Manyo Shukra-mantra-japa-priyah |
Shveta-dhatu-ratna-dash-cha Vajra-mani-prabha-karah || 14 ||

Dashama-sthana-rajya-do Saptame Kama-sukhadah |
Chaturthe Griha-vahana-do Dvitiye Dhana-sampadam || 15 ||

Lagne Kanti-sukha-dash-cha Panchame Putra-sampadam |
Trikone Shubha-dash-chaiva Kendre Sampat-prado Grahah || 16 ||

Ayurveda-vidam shreshtho Rasayana-visharadah |
Chikitsa-shastra-kushalo Sarva-roga-nivarakah || 17 ||

Madhu-Daitya-gurush-chaiva Prahlada-upasita Grahah |
Shiva-bhakto Vishnu-bhakto Brahma-vidya-visharadah || 18 ||

Maha-pandita-sampanno Dirgha-kesho Tapasvinam |
Yoga-siddhi-karo Manyo Dhyana-siddhi-prado Grahah || 19 ||

Sarva-sampat-prado Devo Sarva-saukhya-vivardhanah |
Sarva-kama-prado Manyo Sarva-mangala-karakah || 20 ||

|| Phalashruti ||

Shukra-Sahasra-namnam tu pathanad-yat-phalam labhet |
Saundaryam Dhanam-Aishvaryam Sukham Vivaham-apnuyat ||

Shukravare tu yah pathen-niyamena samahitah |
Shukra-dosha-vinirmuktah Bhoga-aishvaryam-avapnuyat ||

Dampatya-sukham-apnoti Kala-vidyasu nipunah |
Shan-masa-pathanad-bhaktya Sarva-siddhim-avapnuyat ||

|| Iti Shri Skanda-Purana-uktam Shri Shukra Sahasranama Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: हिमकुन्दमृणाल सम कान्ति, दैत्यों के परम गुरु, सर्वशास्त्र प्रवक्ता भार्गव शुक्र को प्रणाम। श्वेतवर्ण, चतुर्बाहु, श्वेतवस्त्र, दण्ड-रुद्राक्ष-कमण्डलु-जपमाला धारी, श्वेताश्व रथारूढ दैत्यगुरु शुक्र को वन्दन। शुक्र, भार्गव, काव्य, दैत्यगुरु, भृगुपुत्र, संजीवनी विद्यापारग, तुला-वृषभ राशीश — ये शुक्र के प्रमुख नाम हैं। कामद, भोगवर्धक, सौन्दर्यकारक, स्त्रीसुखद, दाम्पत्यकारक, विवाहद, कलाप्रिय, वैभवप्रद, धनप्रद शुक्र। शुक्रवार को पठन से शुक्रदोष निवृत्ति, सौन्दर्य, धन, ऐश्वर्य, सुख, विवाहसुख, दाम्पत्यसुख और कलाविद्या में निपुणता प्राप्ति होती है।
benefits: Traditionally recited for removal of shukra dosha and venus afflictions from horoscope
Invokes beauty, wealth, luxury, and material comforts
Invokes the blessing of marital happiness and harmonious relationships
Invokes vehicles, property, and royal luxuries
Invokes the blessing of mastery in arts, music, and creative pursuits
Six months of recitation grants all siddhis
description: Shukra Sahasranama Stotram (श्री शुक्र सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम्) is a sacred recitation of 1000 divine names dedicated to the Nine Planets (Navagraha). This revered text originates from Skanda Purana. Devotees recite this prayer to grant beauty, wealth, luxury, and material comforts and bestow mastery in arts, music, and creative pursuits. It is also said to bestow marital happiness and harmonious relationships. This prayer is traditionally recited on Friday (Shukravar) to invoke divine grace. With 23 verses and a reading time of about 25 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Shukra Sahasranama Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Shukra Sahasranama in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. 1000 names of Venus for wealth, beauty, marriage and artistic success.
relatedSearches: Shukra dosha remedies for marriage and beauty

Shukra graha shanti puja vidhi at home
Diamond gemstone for Venus afflictions astrology

seoFaqs.question: Who is Shukra and what does the Sahasranama address?
seoFaqs.answer: Shukra (also called Bhargava, Kavya, Daityaguru) is the Vedic-Puranic personification of the planet Venus and the preceptor of the asuras. He is the son of sage Bhrigu, possessor of the Mritsanjeevani Vidya (the science of reviving the dead), and one of the nine Navagrahas. The Sahasranama addresses him in his planetary, sage and preceptor roles simultaneously: as Bhargava the descendant of Bhrigu, as Kavya the seer of the Ushanas-samhita, as the white-complexioned, four-armed deity riding a chariot drawn by white horses, and as the karaka of marriage, beauty, vehicles, luxury and the arts.

seoFaqs.question: Where is the Shukra Sahasranama traditionally recorded?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shukra Sahasranama is attributed to the Skanda Purana and is part of the Navagraha Sahasranama corpus alongside the Sahasranamas of the other eight planetary deities. The text opens with a dhyana sloka describing Shukra as resembling snow, kunda flower and lotus stem (hima-kunda-mrinala), the supreme guru of the daityas, the speaker of all shastras. He is depicted holding a danda (staff), rudraksha mala, kamandalu and japa-mala. Several Pancharatra and Jyotisha compendia also reproduce versions of the hymn for use in Shukra-shanti rites and Friday Lakshmi-Shukra puja.

seoFaqs.question: What is Shukra dosha and how does this Sahasranama remedy it?
seoFaqs.answer: Shukra dosha refers to afflictions caused by a malefic, debilitated, retrograde or combust Venus in a person's horoscope. Symptoms include marital discord, delayed marriage, loss of beauty and sensual pleasures, problems with vehicles and luxury, eye and reproductive disorders, and absence of artistic talent. The phalashruti of this Sahasranama promises that Friday recitation in concentrated discipline destroys Shukra dosha and bestows beauty, wealth, lordship, marital pleasures and dampatya-sukha (couple's harmony). Six months of dedicated recitation is held to confer all siddhis. It is typically prescribed alongside Lakshmi worship and Mahalakshmi Ashtakam for full Shukra parihara.

seoFaqs.question: Why is Shukra called the Daityaguru and Mritsanjeevani-vidya bearer?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 2 calls Shukra the bearer of the Sanjivani-Vidya, the Mahabharata story explains how Shukra obtained this from Lord Shiva through severe penance, and used it to revive slain asuras during the Devasura wars. As the asuras' guru, he balanced the deva-side advantage of Brihaspati. Despite serving the daityas, Shukra is one of the Navagraha worshipped equally in Smarta tradition because his planetary office is independent of his political alignment. The Sahasranama therefore praises him as the impartial dispenser of fortunate Friday-graha effects to all jivas.

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Shukra Sahasranama be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Friday (Shukravar) is the principal day, ideally at sunrise or during the Venus hora. White is the recommended dress colour, white flowers (jasmine, kunda, lotus) and curd or milk-based sweets (kheer, payasam) are the offerings. A small white-cloth-covered mandapa with a Shukra Yantra or Lakshmi photograph serves as the focal point. The recitation is traditionally preceded by a sankalpa for specific marital, beauty or wealth concerns. For serious Shukra dosha cases, a 16-Friday or six-month uninterrupted sankalpa is undertaken, often accompanied by a diamond or white sapphire (recommended only after astrological consultation), and donation of white items on Friday.

seoFaqs.question: What other benefits does the Shukra Sahasranama bestow?
seoFaqs.answer: Beyond Shukra dosha relief, the Sahasranama promises beauty (saundarya), wealth (dhana), prosperity and luxury (aishvarya), marital happiness (dampatya-sukha), good vehicles (yana-vahana), gain of property (bhavana-da), expertise in arts including poetry, music, dance and drama (kavya, sangita, nritya), mastery of Ayurveda and Rasayana (verse 17), success in Vedanta study, blessing of progeny, and recovery from blood and reproductive disorders. Verse 18 also identifies Shukra as a great Shiva and Vishnu devotee, which is why his worship is considered to bring the secondary blessings of these two principal deities as well.
